findings.content: A persistent grocery list with substitution preferences reduces cart abandonment by giving users control over the substitution decision before checkout, not during delivery.
findings.source: Prototype testing with 3 synthetic personas (Weekly Basket Builder, Fresh-Obsessed, Quick-Run Shopper)

findings.content: Delivery slot visualization (showing confidence level per slot) is a low-effort, high-impact UX change that could address slot anxiety independently of the broader conversion funnel.
findings.source: Competitive analysis of Instacart and Ocado slot UI patterns

findings.content: A full redesign of the grocery flow risks disrupting existing power users who have adapted to the current friction. Incremental changes may be safer.
findings.source: Experience audit of current grocery flow

artifacts_produced.description: Interactive prototype of persistent grocery list with substitution preferences

self_review.self_check_notes: Verified solution approaches are genuinely different (persistent list vs slot visualization vs incremental UX). Prototype tests a real hypothesis about substitution control.
self_review.review_feedback: Findings are well-grounded in prototype testing. Risk finding about power user disruption is important but needs data validation.
revisions_made: Added confidence levels to each finding
Expanded source attribution for competitive analysis
